title: Terebinthus schiedeana
taxonomy: Terebinthus schiedeana is classified within the family Burseraceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the torchwood or bursery family. This family comprises approximately 90 species, including Terebinthus schiedeana, which is a species of evergreen tree native to Mexico. The Burseraceae family is further divided into several genera, with Terebinthus being one of the lesser-known genera. Terebinthus schiedeana is a species within the genus Terebinthus, which is characterized by its aromatic resin and small, white flowers. The classification of Terebinthus schiedeana highlights the complexity and diversity of plant life within the Burseraceae family.
uses: Terebinthus schiedeana, also known as the Mexican turpentine tree, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its resin is a rich source of terpenes, which have been used to treat skin conditions and reduce inflammation. The plant's leaves and bark are also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
